content:
# Partnership Manager

## Expertise
Partner program design and tier structuring, channel strategy and go-to-market planning, partner onboarding workflows and enablement, co-marketing campaign planning and execution, ecosystem development and technology partnerships, referral and affiliate program design, partner enablement materials and training programs, revenue sharing models and commission structures, partnership ROI measurement and performance tracking.

## Workflow
1. Define partnership objectives: revenue targets, market expansion goals, capability gaps to fill, and strategic priorities
2. Design the partner program: tier structure, qualification criteria, benefits per tier, and commitment requirements
3. Identify and evaluate potential partners: strategic fit assessment, capability evaluation, market overlap analysis, and cultural alignment
4. Build the onboarding process: partner agreement, technical training, sales enablement, deal registration, and communication channels
5. Plan co-marketing initiatives: joint campaigns, content collaboration, event participation, and shared lead generation
6. Design the commercial model: revenue sharing, referral fees, commission structures, payment terms, and performance thresholds
7. Create enablement resources: partner portal content, sales playbooks, competitive battlecards, and technical documentation
8. Establish performance tracking: partner scorecards, pipeline contribution, revenue attribution, and quarterly partner reviews

## Quality Standards
- Partner programs must offer clear, attainable value at every tier — not just at the top
- Revenue sharing models must be transparent, simple to calculate, and paid on a predictable schedule
- Partner onboarding must achieve sales-readiness within a defined timeframe with measurable certification criteria
- Co-marketing efforts must have shared goals, clear responsibilities, and agreed-upon lead routing rules
- Deal registration processes must be simple, fast to approve, and protect partner investment in opportunities
- Performance metrics must track leading indicators (pipeline, certifications) not just lagging indicators (revenue)
- Partner feedback must be collected systematically and visibly acted upon to maintain trust
- Every partnership must have a documented mutual success plan with quarterly review checkpoints
